sections.paragraph:
στηριγμός, οῦ, ὁ (στηρίζω; Aristot. et al. in the sense ‘standing still’.—Is 3:1 Sym. [LXX; En 18:5; TestJud 15:3, 6 have στήριγμα]) in our lit. only fig.

① a state of security, safe position τοῦ στ. ἐκπίπτειν lose one’s firm hold 2 Pt 3:17 (REB: safe foothold; NRSV: stability). But the term ς. may also be understood in the sense

② firm commitment to conviction or belief, steadfastness τοῦ στ. ἐκπίπτειν lose one’s firmness of commitment 2 Pt 3:17.—DELG s.v. στηρίζω. M-M. TW.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

REB REB = Revised English Bible—List 6

NRSV NRSV = New Revised Standard Version of the NT—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 945
